Andheri West is a prominent and vibrant neighbourhood in Mumbai. It has stunning sea-side landscapes and natural beauty, and is more affluent than its eastern counterpart. The bulk of the houses in Andheri West date from the early 1940s, and many of them have a vintage feel to them. It is a neighbourhood for the upperclass, and it is home to numerous TV personalities, which has resulted in skyrocketing house values.Andheri West vs Andheri EastAndheri West is distinct from its eastern counterpart in a number of respects. Andheri East has a lot of industrial exposure and good connections, but it lacks fundamental structure and organisation, which Andheri West has. The western part of this area is known for its lush greenery and tranquil atmosphere, making it a popular real estate choice. Andheri West is mostly a residential area with a variety of amenities to meet the requirements of its people. Cost of living in Andheri West The cost of living in Andheri West is higher compared to other areas of Mumbai. The average rent for a 1-bedroom apartment in Andheri West is around ₹31,000 per month, while the average rent for a 3-bedroom apartment is around ₹58,000. Groceries and other daily expenses are usually expensive, with the average cost for a meal for two people costing around ₹800. Transportation costs are also expensive, with a monthly public transportation pass costing around ₹1,200.

Goregaon East is a suburban locality situated in Mumbai, Maharashtra. Goregaon East is situated on the western side of the Mumbai and the nearby localities are Malad, Borivali, Jogeshwari and Andheri. NH 7 (Western Express Highway) passes through Goregaon East and the famous SV road connects Goregaon to Malad to Andheri. Oracle and many big companies are situated in the Nesco IT Park. Lodha builders and Rahega developers are the major builders in this region. Goregaon railway station is situated within the locality. There is metro construction going for expanding the Andheri metro line to Goregaon on the western Express highway. Oberoi Mall is situated on the Goregaon East and is a famous shopping spot. There is no water scarcity in this locality as the BMC provides 24 hour water supply. Goregaon East doesn’t face any load shedding issues and if there is some maintenance work prior notice is given to the residents. Goregaon is well connected to all major areas of Mumbai by local trains and government buses. Street food near Goregaon railway station is quite famous and has good sitting arrangement.

Santacruz East, Mumbai, is one of the most high-demand localities due to its prime location among the major areas of Mumbai like Bandra, Kurla, Kinala, etc. The convenient access to the neighboring localities and their commercial hubs is the major selling point of Santacruz East. There are many major colleges and universities in this area, like Kalina University and the University of Mumbai. There are also many exceptional schools like St. Mary's High School and Hans Bhugra High School. Major hotel chains like the Hyatt Residences are also present in the locality. Santacruz East also has a lot of temples and churches, many of which are famous for their design and architecture and have gained a lot of popularity among the crowds. The Western Express Highway connects Santacruz East to the neighboring areas. The nearest airport to the locality is the Mumbai International Airport which is a half an hour's drive from Santacruz East. There are many shopping centers and malls in the locality, and the local markets are also always crowded. There are many metro stations close to Santacruz East, and the locality also has several bus stations. There are also several hospitals in the area which offer good healthcare facilities.

Chembur is a suburb of Eastern Mumbai that changed from an industrial suburb to a residential hub over the years since Independence. Chembur is an old and fully developed suburban area located in the Eastern part of Mumbai at a distance of approximately 15km from the central part of the city. The name of this place is believed to have been derived from the Marathi word 'Chimboree' meaning large crab. Chembur was originally on the north-western corner of Trombay Island before the land reclamation project took place. The railway line for Chembur was first opened to passengers in 1924 and was made part of Greater Bombay in 1945. This place also has a history of having hosted refugees after independence and following partition. The growth and gradual development of residential areas occurred after the industrialisation of Trombay during war. Chembur eventually transformed from an industrial suburb to primarily a residential area after the construction of the Township Colony, Shell Colony and Station Colony undertaken by the Bombay Housing Board. Today Chembur is home to many more such colonies like BPCL, Tata Colony, Pestom, Indian Oil Nagar, HPCL, Chedda Nagar, Tilak Nagar etc. One can also find several independent houses in Chembur in combination with modern high rises and plenty of open spaces – something that is so difficult to find in Mumbai.One of the many notable landmarks and one of the biggest green cover areas for this locality is the Bombay Presidency Golf Club. Numerous well-developed suburbs can be seen nearby Chembur locality which includes Kurla, Deonar, Chunabhatti and Ghatkopar. Reasons to invest in Chembur:The building of the monorail, elevated road and the Eastern Freeway has significantly contributed to the fast paced growth of one of Mumbai's greenest suburbs. Reputed builders like Godrej, Ekta Group and Hubtown have come up with residential cum commercial projects in Chembur. Given its location and proximity to the city, any property investment in Chembur is assured to bring in good returns.

Chembur is a suburb of Eastern Mumbai that changed from an industrial suburb to a residential hub over the years since Independence. Chembur is an old and fully developed suburban area located in the Eastern part of Mumbai at a distance of approximately 15km from the central part of the city. The name of this place is believed to have been derived from the Marathi word 'Chimboree' meaning large crab. Chembur was originally on the north-western corner of Trombay Island before the land reclamation project took place. The railway line for Chembur was first opened to passengers in 1924 and was made part of Greater Bombay in 1945. This place also has a history of having hosted refugees after independence and following partition. The growth and gradual development of residential areas occurred after the industrialisation of Trombay during war. Chembur eventually transformed from an industrial suburb to primarily a residential area after the construction of the Township Colony, Shell Colony and Station Colony undertaken by the Bombay Housing Board. Today Chembur is home to many more such colonies like BPCL, Tata Colony, Pestom, Indian Oil Nagar, HPCL, Chedda Nagar, Tilak Nagar etc. One can also find several independent houses in Chembur in combination with modern high rises and plenty of open spaces – something that is so difficult to find in Mumbai.One of the many notable landmarks and one of the biggest green cover areas for this locality is the Bombay Presidency Golf Club. Numerous well-developed suburbs can be seen nearby Chembur locality which includes Kurla, Deonar, Chunabhatti and Ghatkopar. Reasons to invest in Chembur:The building of the monorail, elevated road and the Eastern Freeway has significantly contributed to the fast paced growth of one of Mumbai's greenest suburbs. Reputed builders like Godrej, Ekta Group and Hubtown have come up with residential cum commercial projects in Chembur. Given its location and proximity to the city, any property investment in Chembur is assured to bring in good returns.

Ghatkopar East is situated in the heart of Mumbai city and is one of the most developed localities in the area. The pin code of Ghatkopar East is 400077 and postal head office is Rajawadi. Raja Wadi, Neelkanth Chhaya Co-op Housing Society, Maheshwar Nagar, Mg Road are the nearby localities to Ghatkopar East. The famous Phoenix Market City is located in Ghatkopar. The locality has no load sheading issues or any water scarcity issues. Ghatkopar East is considered as a prime locality in Mumbai and many of the film stars and other successful people reside in this neighbourhood. The first Mumbai metro project is from Ghatkopar to Andheri. Ghatkopar railway station is situated within the locality. Many top commercial and industrial companies are situated in this region. Schools and colleges are at close proximity from Ghatkopar East. Marathi is the local language in this region, people also speak and understand English and Hindi. Banks and ATM`s are situated at a walking distance here. The locality has many religious places with Ganesh mandir being the most visited one at any time of the year.

Locality Guide
for Malad East, Mumbai
Share
+
56 More
Malad East is one of the key locations before Andheri on the Western Suburban Line. Malad East has developed as a medium residential location favoring working professionals working in the Andheri region up to Borivali. Towards its east is Sanjay Gandhi National Park, Goregaon West in the south, and Kandivali East in the north. Triveni Nagar, Mira Nagar, and Kokanipada are a few nearby localities. Grace Serene by Grace Group, Neev Galaxy by Neev Group, and Vora Ashish by Vora Group are a few key residential complexes in this locality. Malad is well connected to the rest of the city through the Malad Railway Station on the Western Line and the Western Expressway. Mumbai International Airport is approximately 10 km away. Also, the proposed Malad Metro Station on Line 2, tentatively scheduled to open in 2020, is expected to improve the area's connectivity and increase residential demand in the locality. The approximate population is around 946,000, with an additional daily floating population of 100,000. Malad as a whole (East and West area) falls under the P-North ward, which is the most populous ward in the city as per the 2011 census. The suburb has a large Gujarati population selling clothes, food, etc., in the area, and a few of them have photocopiers and stationery shops.

Malad East is one of the key locations before Andheri on the Western Suburban Line. Malad East has developed as a medium residential location favoring working professionals working in the Andheri region up to Borivali. Towards its east is Sanjay Gandhi National Park, Goregaon West in the south, and Kandivali East in the north. Triveni Nagar, Mira Nagar, and Kokanipada are a few nearby localities. Grace Serene by Grace Group, Neev Galaxy by Neev Group, and Vora Ashish by Vora Group are a few key residential complexes in this locality. Malad is well connected to the rest of the city through the Malad Railway Station on the Western Line and the Western Expressway. Mumbai International Airport is approximately 10 km away. Also, the proposed Malad Metro Station on Line 2, tentatively scheduled to open in 2020, is expected to improve the area's connectivity and increase residential demand in the locality. The approximate population is around 946,000, with an additional daily floating population of 100,000. Malad as a whole (East and West area) falls under the P-North ward, which is the most populous ward in the city as per the 2011 census. The suburb has a large Gujarati population selling clothes, food, etc., in the area, and a few of them have photocopiers and stationery shops.

Andheri East is a well-developed residential as well as commercial locality situated along the Western Express highway. Its surrounding localities are Powai, Vile Parle, Andheri West, and Goregaon East. Andheri East is known for providing 1-2 BHK housing units, due to which it witnesses demand from middle and upper-middle-income segment homebuyers. SEEPZ and MIDC are the prominent industrial areas in Andheri East. Western express highway boosts the connectivity of the locality as it connects Andheri East to commercial hubs such as Bandra Kurla Complex, Powai, and Lower Parel. It also provides easy connectivity from Andheri East to Malad West, Kandivali West, and Borivali. Andheri railway station is just 2.1km away. Andheri Metro station is also 2km away, which further enhances the connectivity of Andheri East. Proximity to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj international airport is an added benefit. All the above factors boost the property rates of the locality. Andheri East is well surrounded by basic amenities like reputed schools, multi-speciality hospitals, shopping malls, parks, hotels, and restaurants. Planned Metro extension will further boost the connectivity of the locality. Mahakali caves, Andheri Sports Complex, Holy Family Church, are some of the known tourist spots in Andheri East. Cost of living in Andheri East The average cost of living in Andheri East is around ₹50,000 to ₹70,000 per month, depending on your lifestyle. Rent in the area can range from ₹15,000 to ₹50,000 per month, depending on the size and location of the accommodation. Eating out, groceries, transportation, and other daily expenses can also add up to ₹20,000 to ₹30,000 per month.

Locality Guide
for Andheri East, Mumbai
Share
Locality Overview Video
Andheri East is a well-developed residential as well as commercial locality situated along the Western Express highway. Its surrounding localities are Powai, Vile Parle, Andheri West, and Goregaon East. Andheri East is known for providing 1-2 BHK housing units, due to which it witnesses demand from middle and upper-middle-income segment homebuyers. SEEPZ and MIDC are the prominent industrial areas in Andheri East. Western express highway boosts the connectivity of the locality as it connects Andheri East to commercial hubs such as Bandra Kurla Complex, Powai, and Lower Parel. It also provides easy connectivity from Andheri East to Malad West, Kandivali West, and Borivali. Andheri railway station is just 2.1km away. Andheri Metro station is also 2km away, which further enhances the connectivity of Andheri East. Proximity to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj international airport is an added benefit. All the above factors boost the property rates of the locality. Andheri East is well surrounded by basic amenities like reputed schools, multi-speciality hospitals, shopping malls, parks, hotels, and restaurants. Planned Metro extension will further boost the connectivity of the locality. Mahakali caves, Andheri Sports Complex, Holy Family Church, are some of the known tourist spots in Andheri East. Cost of living in Andheri East The average cost of living in Andheri East is around ₹50,000 to ₹70,000 per month, depending on your lifestyle. Rent in the area can range from ₹15,000 to ₹50,000 per month, depending on the size and location of the accommodation. Eating out, groceries, transportation, and other daily expenses can also add up to ₹20,000 to ₹30,000 per month.

Locality Guide
for Andheri East, Mumbai

Locality Overview Video
Andheri East is a well-developed residential as well as commercial locality situated along the Western Express highway. Its surrounding localities are Powai, Vile Parle, Andheri West, and Goregaon East. Andheri East is known for providing 1-2 BHK housing units, due to which it witnesses demand from middle and upper-middle-income segment homebuyers. SEEPZ and MIDC are the prominent industrial areas in Andheri East. Western express highway boosts the connectivity of the locality as it connects Andheri East to commercial hubs such as Bandra Kurla Complex, Powai, and Lower Parel. It also provides easy connectivity from Andheri East to Malad West, Kandivali West, and Borivali. Andheri railway station is just 2.1km away. Andheri Metro station is also 2km away, which further enhances the connectivity of Andheri East. Proximity to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj international airport is an added benefit. All the above factors boost the property rates of the locality. Andheri East is well surrounded by basic amenities like reputed schools, multi-speciality hospitals, shopping malls, parks, hotels, and restaurants. Planned Metro extension will further boost the connectivity of the locality. Mahakali caves, Andheri Sports Complex, Holy Family Church, are some of the known tourist spots in Andheri East. Cost of living in Andheri East The average cost of living in Andheri East is around ₹50,000 to ₹70,000 per month, depending on your lifestyle. Rent in the area can range from ₹15,000 to ₹50,000 per month, depending on the size and location of the accommodation. Eating out, groceries, transportation, and other daily expenses can also add up to ₹20,000 to ₹30,000 per month.

Virar West offers a contrasting atmosphere to the bustling energy of Mumbai. Virar West offers high-quality amenities, including modern, reputable malls, hospitals, and excellent schools. Virar West boasts of schools of all categories, including SSC, CBSE, IGCSE, ISC, and more, ensuring that families have access to diverse educational options tailored to their needs. Alongside high-quality malls and reputable hospitals, the locality also offers close proximity to beautiful beaches, from Arnala Beach to Kalamb Beach. Virar West is one of the oldest towns of the state of Maharashtra located in the Palghar district. Being one of the oldest areas of the state, it is well planned and has a cosmopolitan population. The locality comes under the purview of the Mumbai Metropolitan region and is very well connected to all the parts of the City of Mumbai.Virar West is one of the oldest cities of India located in very close proximity to the city of Mumbai. The area houses many temples, churches, resorts and amusement parks. Paragliding can also be done in the area. Virar has also some of the heritage sites of the country. Nearby Virar West localities include Vasai, Mira road, Alibaug, Vatar and Navapur etc. Being one of the oldest cities it is one of the premier residential areas as far as Virar West real estate is concerned. This area offers a great connectivity to major landmarks of Mumbai. As a result of this there has been a surge in the demand for residential apartments in Virar West, being one of the noted suburbs of Mumbai.There are a number of noted projects which have come up in the area like Mayfair Housing Primrose, Mayfair Housing Virar Gardens, and Rustomjee Avenue I etc. Builders in Virar West includes famed developers like the Mayfair Housing Group, Bhoomi Group, Rustomjee Group, who are working on some of the projects in Virar West.Why Invest in Virar West?Considering the price trends in Virar West and the rising demand, the locality should prove to be a good investment. There is excellent appreciation of property rates and with a number of projects which are being developed. The area lies in close proximity to the city of Mumbai and offers excellent connectivity and social infrastructure and these are reasons for its rapid growth. There is no denying that Virar West is certainly one of the best places to live close to Mumbai. Considering all these factors Virar West is an excellent place for investment.

Andheri East is a well-developed residential as well as commercial locality situated along the Western Express highway. Its surrounding localities are Powai, Vile Parle, Andheri West, and Goregaon East. Andheri East is known for providing 1-2 BHK housing units, due to which it witnesses demand from middle and upper-middle-income segment homebuyers. SEEPZ and MIDC are the prominent industrial areas in Andheri East. Western express highway boosts the connectivity of the locality as it connects Andheri East to commercial hubs such as Bandra Kurla Complex, Powai, and Lower Parel. It also provides easy connectivity from Andheri East to Malad West, Kandivali West, and Borivali. Andheri railway station is just 2.1km away. Andheri Metro station is also 2km away, which further enhances the connectivity of Andheri East. Proximity to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj international airport is an added benefit. All the above factors boost the property rates of the locality. Andheri East is well surrounded by basic amenities like reputed schools, multi-speciality hospitals, shopping malls, parks, hotels, and restaurants. Planned Metro extension will further boost the connectivity of the locality. Mahakali caves, Andheri Sports Complex, Holy Family Church, are some of the known tourist spots in Andheri East. Cost of living in Andheri East The average cost of living in Andheri East is around ₹50,000 to ₹70,000 per month, depending on your lifestyle. Rent in the area can range from ₹15,000 to ₹50,000 per month, depending on the size and location of the accommodation. Ghatkopar East is situated in the heart of Mumbai city and is one of the most developed localities in the area. The pin code of Ghatkopar East is 400077 and postal head office is Rajawadi. Raja Wadi, Neelkanth Chhaya Co-op Housing Society, Maheshwar Nagar, Mg Road are the nearby localities to Ghatkopar East. The famous Phoenix Market City is located in Ghatkopar. The locality has no load sheading issues or any water scarcity issues. Ghatkopar East is considered as a prime locality in Mumbai and many of the film stars and other successful people reside in this neighbourhood. The first Mumbai metro project is from Ghatkopar to Andheri. Ghatkopar railway station is situated within the locality. Many top commercial and industrial companies are situated in this region. Schools and colleges are at close proximity from Ghatkopar East. Marathi is the local language in this region, people also speak and understand English and Hindi. Banks and ATM`s are situated at a walking distance here. The locality has many religious places with Ganesh mandir being the most visited one at any time of the year.

Chembur is a suburb of Eastern Mumbai that changed from an industrial suburb to a residential hub over the years since Independence. Chembur is an old and fully developed suburban area located in the Eastern part of Mumbai at a distance of approximately 15km from the central part of the city. The name of this place is believed to have been derived from the Marathi word 'Chimboree' meaning large crab. Chembur was originally on the north-western corner of Trombay Island before the land reclamation project took place. The railway line for Chembur was first opened to passengers in 1924 and was made part of Greater Bombay in 1945. This place also has a history of having hosted refugees after independence and following partition. The growth and gradual development of residential areas occurred after the industrialisation of Trombay during war. Chembur eventually transformed from an industrial suburb to primarily a residential area after the construction of the Township Colony, Shell Colony and Station Colony undertaken by the Bombay Housing Board. Today Chembur is home to many more such colonies like BPCL, Tata Colony, Pestom, Indian Oil Nagar, HPCL, Chedda Nagar, Tilak Nagar etc. One can also find several independent houses in Chembur in combination with modern high rises and plenty of open spaces – something that is so difficult to find in Mumbai.One of the many notable landmarks and one of the biggest green cover areas for this locality is the Bombay Presidency Golf Club. Numerous well-developed suburbs can be seen nearby Chembur locality which includes Kurla, Deonar, Chunabhatti and Ghatkopar. Reasons to invest in Chembur:The building of the monorail, elevated road and the Eastern Freeway has significantly contributed to the fast paced growth of one of Mumbai's greenest suburbs. Reputed builders like Godrej, Ekta Group and Hubtown have come up with residential cum commercial projects in Chembur. Given its location and proximity to the city, any property investment in Chembur is assured to bring in good returns.

Ghatkopar East is situated in the heart of Mumbai city and is one of the most developed localities in the area. The pin code of Ghatkopar East is 400077 and postal head office is Rajawadi. Raja Wadi, Neelkanth Chhaya Co-op Housing Society, Maheshwar Nagar, Mg Road are the nearby localities to Ghatkopar East. The famous Phoenix Market City is located in Ghatkopar. The locality has no load sheading issues or any water scarcity issues. Ghatkopar East is considered as a prime locality in Mumbai and many of the film stars and other successful people reside in this neighbourhood. The first Mumbai metro project is from Ghatkopar to Andheri. Ghatkopar railway station is situated within the locality. Many top commercial and industrial companies are situated in this region. Schools and colleges are at close proximity from Ghatkopar East. Marathi is the local language in this region, people also speak and understand English and Hindi. Banks and ATM`s are situated at a walking distance here. The locality has many religious places with Ganesh mandir being the most visited one at any time of the year.

Ghatkopar East is situated in the heart of Mumbai city and is one of the most developed localities in the area. The pin code of Ghatkopar East is 400077 and postal head office is Rajawadi. Raja Wadi, Neelkanth Chhaya Co-op Housing Society, Maheshwar Nagar, Mg Road are the nearby localities to Ghatkopar East. The famous Phoenix Market City is located in Ghatkopar. The locality has no load sheading issues or any water scarcity issues. Ghatkopar East is considered as a prime locality in Mumbai and many of the film stars and other successful people reside in this neighbourhood. The first Mumbai metro project is from Ghatkopar to Andheri. Ghatkopar railway station is situated within the locality. Many top commercial and industrial companies are situated in this region. Schools and colleges are at close proximity from Ghatkopar East. Marathi is the local language in this region, people also speak and understand English and Hindi. Banks and ATM`s are situated at a walking distance here. The locality has many religious places with Ganesh mandir being the most visited one at any time of the year.

Ghatkopar East is situated in the heart of Mumbai city and is one of the most developed localities in the area. The pin code of Ghatkopar East is 400077 and postal head office is Rajawadi. Raja Wadi, Neelkanth Chhaya Co-op Housing Society, Maheshwar Nagar, Mg Road are the nearby localities to Ghatkopar East. The famous Phoenix Market City is located in Ghatkopar. The locality has no load sheading issues or any water scarcity issues. Ghatkopar East is considered as a prime locality in Mumbai and many of the film stars and other successful people reside in this neighbourhood. The first Mumbai metro project is from Ghatkopar to Andheri. Ghatkopar railway station is situated within the locality. Many top commercial and industrial companies are situated in this region. Schools and colleges are at close proximity from Ghatkopar East. Marathi is the local language in this region, people also speak and understand English and Hindi. Banks and ATM`s are situated at a walking distance here. The locality has many religious places with Ganesh mandir being the most visited one at any time of the year.

Locality Guide
for Wadala, Mumbai
Share
+
48 More
Wadala is known for being one of the seven islands of Bombay which combined together form the great city Mumbai. Earlier Wadala was called Parel, Matunga, Dharavi etc. This place comes under Mumbai Municipal Corporation and covers the area of 6.52 sq. km. People here experience hot and humid weather with powerful monsoons. Wadala has estimated population of 143064 and the male and female populations are 74526 and 68538 respectively. The coordinates of this place are of 19.02163°N 72.86459°E. Marathi, Hindi and English are widely spoken however, Marathi is the official language here. Infrastructure and Details As Wadala is considered as the main centre of Mumbai, it is surrounded by Dadar on the West, Matunga on the Northwest, and Sewri on the South. As a part of Mumbai this place is also the home of old temples, Universities and the famous IMAX dome theatre (4.7km), formerly known as world's largest theatre. Wadala has a very good connectivity of trains for Harbour, central and western line. If you walk for 10-15 minutes you will reach to Wadala Railway station (1.8km) and Sion Railway station (5.6km). Nearest airport is Chhatrapati Shivaji International Airport which is approximately 6.17 km away from Wadala. This area is known as the hub of educational institutions like The Andhra Education Society High school (2.1km), Sitaram Prakash High School (2.0km), Veermata Jijabai Technological Institute (1.8km) and Coommo Jafar College (3.0km) etc are accompanied by hospitals like The Ackworth Leprosy Hospital (1.8km), Mumbai Port Trust Hospital (270m), Sengol Hospital (1.3km) and RSV Hospital (900m). Major employment centers include the Indiabulls Finance Center (5.1km), Peninsula Business Park (6.3km), Kamala Mills Compound (6.4km) etc. Moreover, Wadala is also connected with commercial centres and government offices like Bandra Kurla Complex (10km), Kanakia Zillion (11km), Mumbai Port Trust (10km) etc. Property in Wadala As we know Mumbai offers a wide mix of apartments and alike. So does Wadala, here you can find apartments, studio apartments, luxury villas, cottages, independent houses in different configurations from 1-5 BHK, you can also find larger apartments here. Any apartment starts from 200-230 sq. ft and increases up to 30,000 sq. ft. There are number of PMAY and MHADA housing schemes that take care of the housing projects here.

Vasai West is one of the most prominent residential hubs in the western part of Mumbai due to its affordable rates. It provides a range of high, low, and mid-rise apartments as well as individual homes. Since the rental rates are so affordable, it is highly suitable for young migrant professionals. The locality is beautiful and surrounded by greenery. Virar, Nalasopara, Naigoan, and Mira-Bhayandar are some of the localities surrounding Vasai West. Vasai Railway station is just 5 km away and the Western express highway connects Vasai West to major parts of Mumbai. The locality is preferred mostly because all basic amenities are nearby. Some of the best schools, hospitals, parks, markets, malls are very nearby. Public transport is provided in the locality by Vasai Virar Transport Service(VVTS)

Andheri East is a well-developed residential as well as commercial locality situated along the Western Express highway. Its surrounding localities are Powai, Vile Parle, Andheri West, and Goregaon East. Andheri East is known for providing 1-2 BHK housing units, due to which it witnesses demand from middle and upper-middle-income segment homebuyers. SEEPZ and MIDC are the prominent industrial areas in Andheri East. Western express highway boosts the connectivity of the locality as it connects Andheri East to commercial hubs such as Bandra Kurla Complex, Powai, and Lower Parel. It also provides easy connectivity from Andheri East to Malad West, Kandivali West, and Borivali. Andheri railway station is just 2.1km away. Andheri Metro station is also 2km away, which further enhances the connectivity of Andheri East. Proximity to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj international airport is an added benefit. All the above factors boost the property rates of the locality. Andheri East is well surrounded by basic amenities like reputed schools, multi-speciality hospitals, shopping malls, parks, hotels, and restaurants. Planned Metro extension will further boost the connectivity of the locality. Mahakali caves, Andheri Sports Complex, Holy Family Church, are some of the known tourist spots in Andheri East. Cost of living in Andheri East The average cost of living in Andheri East is around ₹50,000 to ₹70,000 per month, depending on your lifestyle. Rent in the area can range from ₹15,000 to ₹50,000 per month, depending on the size and location of the accommodation. Eating out, groceries, transportation, and other daily expenses can also add up to ₹20,000 to ₹30,000 per month.

Malad West, a prominent and upscale locality of Mumbai, is situated on the western line of the Mumbai Suburban Railway. It is a green and serene locality situated along Malad Creek and surrounded by mangroves. The residential development in Malad West majorly comprises multi-store apartments and villas. It is driven by proximity to Mindspace and other IT parks and excellent connectivity to other parts of Mumbai. Also, it is located adjacent to other prominent locations in western Mumbai, such as Malad East, Kandivali West, and Goregaon West. In addition, Malad West has good connectivity to the significant areas of west and southern Mumbai, Navi Mumbai, and Mira-Bhayander through the western and harbor lines, SV Road, New Link Road, and the Western Express Highway. Further, it has easy access to Jogeshwari Vikhroli Link Road (JVLR) and Aarey Road. Moreover, Mumbai International Airport is located 12-17 km from Malad West. All prestigious educational institutions are located in the vicinity of the locality. Goregaon Sports Club, Green Village Recreation Club & Pool, and Malad Gymkhana are key recreational facilities in and around Malad West. Ghatkopar East is situated in the heart of Mumbai city and is one of the most developed localities in the area. The pin code of Ghatkopar East is 400077 and postal head office is Rajawadi. Raja Wadi, Neelkanth Chhaya Co-op Housing Society, Maheshwar Nagar, Mg Road are the nearby localities to Ghatkopar East. The famous Phoenix Market City is located in Ghatkopar. The locality has no load sheading issues or any water scarcity issues. Ghatkopar East is considered as a prime locality in Mumbai and many of the film stars and other successful people reside in this neighbourhood. The first Mumbai metro project is from Ghatkopar to Andheri. Ghatkopar railway station is situated within the locality. Many top commercial and industrial companies are situated in this region. Schools and colleges are at close proximity from Ghatkopar East. Marathi is the local language in this region, people also speak and understand English and Hindi. Banks and ATM`s are situated at a walking distance here. The locality has many religious places with Ganesh mandir being the most visited one at any time of the year.

Andheri East is a well-developed residential as well as commercial locality situated along the Western Express highway. Its surrounding localities are Powai, Vile Parle, Andheri West, and Goregaon East. Andheri East is known for providing 1-2 BHK housing units, due to which it witnesses demand from middle and upper-middle-income segment homebuyers. SEEPZ and MIDC are the prominent industrial areas in Andheri East. Western express highway boosts the connectivity of the locality as it connects Andheri East to commercial hubs such as Bandra Kurla Complex, Powai, and Lower Parel. It also provides easy connectivity from Andheri East to Malad West, Kandivali West, and Borivali. Andheri railway station is just 2.1km away. Andheri Metro station is also 2km away, which further enhances the connectivity of Andheri East. Proximity to Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj international airport is an added benefit. All the above factors boost the property rates of the locality. Andheri East is well surrounded by basic amenities like reputed schools, multi-speciality hospitals, shopping malls, parks, hotels, and restaurants. Planned Metro extension will further boost the connectivity of the locality. Mahakali caves, Andheri Sports Complex, Holy Family Church, are some of the known tourist spots in Andheri East. Cost of living in Andheri East The average cost of living in Andheri East is around ₹50,000 to ₹70,000 per month, depending on your lifestyle. Rent in the area can range from ₹15,000 to ₹50,000 per month, depending on the size and location of the accommodation. Eating out, groceries, transportation, and other daily expenses can also add up to ₹20,000 to ₹30,000 per month.

Goregaon West, which was once in the outskirts of Mumbai, is now a part of Mumbai. It is on the northwestern fringe and is right next to western Express Highway and also alongside the Western local coastline that runs from Churchgate right up to Virar. The area is developing fast and has developed into both a commercial as well as a residential area. The localities nearby include Piramal Nagar, Unnat Nagar, Prakash Nagar, and Jawahar Nagar. there are IT hubs and business parks in the vicinity. There are a large number of residential projects in Goregaon west include Kalpataru Radiance, Wadhwa Anmol Fortune, Ekta Tripolis, Galaxy Royale, and the Luxor. Some of the well-known builders with prestigious projects in Goregaon West are Kalpataru, the Wadhwa group, Kolte Patil Developers, the Oxford Group, and the Chandak group. 2 BHK and 3 BHK apartments are the most common type of accommodation available in Goregaon West and consist of 77% of all the property available here. Rates range from 22000 to 27000 per square foot, which is high. Buying property at Goregaon West can be a good investment.

Schools and colleges are at close proximity from Ghatkopar East. Marathi is the local language in this region, people also speak and understand English and Hindi.
